introSection:
Millennium Force is an Intamin built giga roller coaster located at Cedar Point in Sandusky, Ohio, USA. It was the park's fourteenth roller coaster since Blue Streak in 1964. When it opened on May 13, 2000, it was the first complete circuit roller coaster over tall and was briefly the tallest full-circuit roller coaster in the world. It was surpassed by Steel Dragon 2000 at Nagashima Spa Land in August 2000.

Millennium Force receives consistently high ratings from the Golden Ticket Awards. It switched between first and second place with Superman The Ride at Six Flags New England until 2016, when it was beaten by Fury 325 at Carowinds.

The ride is marketed with the slogan "The future is riding on it".

sections.title: History
sections.content:
On July 2, 1999, Cedar Fair filed a trademark for the name Millennium Force.

The roller coaster was announced on July 22, 1999. It was the first major coaster built to take advantage of the internet, with both a teaser video and construction updates released onto the web.

Construction of Millennium Force began in August 1999 with land clearing. The ride's highest point was topped off in January 2000.

Millennium Force opened to the media on May 11, 2000, two days prior to the official opening on May 13.

On September 2, 2001, Millennium Force was closed after one of the cables separated, leaving 30 employees stranded above the ground for an hour.

In 2025, the ride received new updates for its 25th anniversary, such as a repaint, new LED lighting and a new lift motor.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Millennium Force uses a cable lift hill rather than a more traditional chain lift. While cable lift hills had been used on smaller roller coasters, this was the first time this technology had been used on a large scale.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: Riders are held in by individual seatbelts and lap-bars. The trains are colored red, blue, and yellow with the Millennium Force logo on the front.

sections.title: Station
sections.content: Millennium Force has separate areas for loading and unloading. Within the station there is a dedicated queue for the front row.

sections.title: Ride experience
sections.content: The catch car for Millennium Force's cable lift carries the train up the hill at a 45-degree angle in a little over 20 seconds by latching onto the middle of the train. The train then drops at an 80-degree angle, reaching a top speed of. It then climbs a overbanked turn at 122 degrees. Then the train travels through the first tunnel and over a parabolic airtime hill, passing over a lagoon and down onto Millennium Island. While on Millennium Island, the train completes an, 360-degree helix, followed by a left overbanked turn at 122 degrees. Then, riders pass through a small right-hand turn before traveling over another airtime hill to exit the island. The train then travels over a smaller airtime hill and into another tunnel, right next to the first tunnel from earlier in the ride. A final airtime hill, right next to the queue line, is crested. The train finishes the ride by traveling through a overbanked turn over the queue and stops at the brake run. Riders exit the train at the exit platform, and the train then returns to the station.

sections.title: Rankings and records
sections.content: When Millennium Force opened, it broke 11 world records:

sections.title: Coaster records
sections.content:
* 1) First ever complete circuit roller coaster to top
 * 2) Tallest complete circuit roller coaster
 * 3) Longest drop on a complete circuit roller coaster
 * 4) Fastest complete circuit roller coaster
 * 5) Steepest non-inversion banked turn on a roller coaster (122°)
 * 6) First roller coaster to feature a magnetic brake system

sections.title: Park records
sections.content:
* 1) Most rides at an amusement park (68/75)
 * 2) Most roller coasters at an amusement park (14/17)
 * 3) Most complete-circuit roller coasters over high at an amusement park (first park to have more than one) (2/5)
 * 4) Most steel roller coasters at an amusement park (12/15)
 * 5) Most feet of roller coaster track at an amusement park (/)

Note: (Record at 2000/Current Statistic)

sections.title: Trivia
sections.content:
* Part of the area of the park occupied only by Millennium Force's track is known as "Millennium Island."
 * This coaster is one of the few that requires riders to buy lockers prior to entering the queue to store loose items.
 * During "Halloweekends," some sections of the track travel through mist.
 * In theme park sim game Planet Coaster, the giga coaster Invincible is based on Millennium Force.

fullText:
Millennium Force is an Intamin built giga roller coaster located at Cedar Point in Sandusky, Ohio, USA. It was the park's fourteenth roller coaster since Blue Streak in 1964. When it opened on May 13, 2000, it was the first complete circuit roller coaster over tall and was briefly the tallest full-circuit roller coaster in the world. It was surpassed by Steel Dragon 2000 at Nagashima Spa Land in August 2000.

Millennium Force receives consistently high ratings from the Golden Ticket Awards. It switched between first and second place with Superman The Ride at Six Flags New England until 2016, when it was beaten by Fury 325 at Carowinds.

The ride is marketed with the slogan "The future is riding on it".

On July 2, 1999, Cedar Fair filed a trademark for the name Millennium Force.

The roller coaster was announced on July 22, 1999. It was the first major coaster built to take advantage of the internet, with both a teaser video and construction updates released onto the web.

Construction of Millennium Force began in August 1999 with land clearing. The ride's highest point was topped off in January 2000.

Millennium Force opened to the media on May 11, 2000, two days prior to the official opening on May 13.

On September 2, 2001, Millennium Force was closed after one of the cables separated, leaving 30 employees stranded above the ground for an hour.

In 2025, the ride received new updates for its 25th anniversary, such as a repaint, new LED lighting and a new lift motor.

Millennium Force uses a cable lift hill rather than a more traditional chain lift. While cable lift hills had been used on smaller roller coasters, this was the first time this technology had been used on a large scale.

Riders are held in by individual seatbelts and lap-bars. The trains are colored red, blue, and yellow with the Millennium Force logo on the front.

Blue track and light-grey supports.

Millennium Force has separate areas for loading and unloading. Within the station there is a dedicated queue for the front row.

The catch car for Millennium Force's cable lift carries the train up the hill at a 45-degree angle in a little over 20 seconds by latching onto the middle of the train. The train then drops at an 80-degree angle, reaching a top speed of. It then climbs a overbanked turn at 122 degrees. Then the train travels through the first tunnel and over a parabolic airtime hill, passing over a lagoon and down onto Millennium Island. While on Millennium Island, the train completes an, 360-degree helix, followed by a left overbanked turn at 122 degrees. Then, riders pass through a small right-hand turn before traveling over another airtime hill to exit the island. The train then travels over a smaller airtime hill and into another tunnel, right next to the first tunnel from earlier in the ride. A final airtime hill, right next to the queue line, is crested. The train finishes the ride by traveling through a overbanked turn over the queue and stops at the brake run. Riders exit the train at the exit platform, and the train then returns to the station.

When Millennium Force opened, it broke 11 world records:


 * 1) First ever complete circuit roller coaster to top
 * 2) Tallest complete circuit roller coaster
 * 3) Longest drop on a complete circuit roller coaster
 * 4) Fastest complete circuit roller coaster
 * 5) Steepest non-inversion banked turn on a roller coaster (122°)
 * 6) First roller coaster to feature a magnetic brake system


 * 1) Most rides at an amusement park (68/75)
 * 2) Most roller coasters at an amusement park (14/17)
 * 3) Most complete-circuit roller coasters over high at an amusement park (first park to have more than one) (2/5)
 * 4) Most steel roller coasters at an amusement park (12/15)
 * 5) Most feet of roller coaster track at an amusement park (/)

Note: (Record at 2000/Current Statistic)


 * Part of the area of the park occupied only by Millennium Force's track is known as "Millennium Island."
 * This coaster is one of the few that requires riders to buy lockers prior to entering the queue to store loose items.
 * During "Halloweekends," some sections of the track travel through mist.
 * In theme park sim game Planet Coaster, the giga coaster Invincible is based on Millennium Force.
